text: The surgeon applies an omental gram patch to the appendiceal stump, using a minimally invasive technique.
Three trocars are placed in a vertical line along the left rectus abdominis muscle, with the center incision at the level of the umbilicus, and additional incisions positioned above and below.
The surgeon applies an omental patch over the appendiceal stump to reinforce the closure, using the omentum to cover and protect the area.

The surgeon places interrupted vicryl sutures over the purse string suture to reinforce the closure of the appendiceal stump. Two O-silk sutures are positioned at the superior and inferior base of the stump, tying them over the omental patch to achieve solid approximation of the omentum to the inflamed stump.
